definitions.text: A particle used for marking the following verb as an infinitive.
examples: He asked me what to do.

definitions.text: As above, with the verb implied.
examples: "Did you visit the museum?" "I wanted to, but it was closed."
If he hasn't read it yet, he ought to.

definitions.text: Used to indicate an obligation on the part of, or a directive given to, the subject.
examples: You are to go to the store and buy a bottle of milk.
I went to the shops to buy some bread.

definitions.text: Indicating destination or final position: In the direction of, so as to arrive at or reach.
examples: We are walking to the shop.
The water came right to the top of this wall.

definitions.text: Used to indicate the target or recipient of an action.
examples: I gave the book to him.

definitions.text: Toward a closed, touching, or engaging position.
A filler word common amongst urban Indians.
examples: I am to so bored right now.
